---
title: "plateau-architectures-740 — analysis"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

# Plateau architectures (DotaML v4–v6 mirror)

Quarto notebook for post-run analysis. Populated after `run_all.py`
completes. Outputs (plots, tables) live in `results/`.

```{python}
#| label: load-metrics
import json
from pathlib import Path

EXP = Path(".").resolve()
metrics = json.loads((EXP / "metrics.json").read_text()) if (EXP / "metrics.json").stat().st_size else {}
metrics.get("comparison_table", "(no run yet)")
```

## Per-arch learning curves

```{python}
#| label: plot-learning
#| eval: false
import json, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
for arch in ["simple_ffn", "residual_ffn", "transformer"]:
    h_path = Path(f"results/{arch}_history.json")
    if not h_path.exists():
        continue
    h = json.loads(h_path.read_text())
    epochs = [r["epoch"] for r in h]
    val_auc = [r["val_auc"] for r in h]
    ax.plot(epochs, val_auc, label=arch, marker="o", lw=1.5)
ax.set_xlabel("epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("val_auc")
ax.axhline(0.6161, color="k", ls="--", lw=1, label="LightGBM baseline")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig("results/learning_curves.png", dpi=110)
fig
```

## Pairwise rank-order check

```{python}
#| label: rank-order
#| eval: false
metrics["pair_gaps"]
```
